 {"id":"bd-2vh3.3","title":"Tier 2: Basic bd mol squash command","description":"Add bd mol squash command for basic molecule execution compression.

## Command

bd mol squash <molecule-id> [flags]
  --dry-run         Preview what would be squashed
  --keep-children   Don't delete ephemeral children after squash
  --json            JSON output

## Implementation

1. Find all ephemeral children of molecule (parent-child deps)
2. Concatenate child descriptions/notes into digest
3. Create digest issue in main repo with:
   - Title: 'Molecule Execution Summary: <original-title>'
   - digest_of: [list of squashed child IDs]
   - ephemeral: false (digest is permanent)
4. Delete ephemeral children (unless --keep-children)
5. Link digest to parent work item

## Schema Changes

Add to Issue struct:
- SquashedAt *time.Time
- SquashDigest string (ID of digest)
- DigestOf []string (IDs of squashed children)

## Acceptance Criteria

- bd mol squash <id> creates digest, removes children
- --dry-run shows preview
- Digest has proper metadata linking","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:57:48.338114-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:53:58.974433-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T13:53:58.974433-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented bd mol squash command with tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:57:48.338636-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:22.601321-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vh3.4","title":"Tier 3: AI-powered squash summarization","description":"## Design: Agent-Provided Summarization (Inversion of Control)

bd is a tool FOR agents, not an agent itself. The calling agent provides
the summary; bd just stores it.

### API

```bash
# Agent generates summary, passes to bd
bd mol squash bd-xxx --summary "Agent-generated summary here"

# Without --summary, falls back to basic concatenation
bd mol squash bd-xxx
```

### Gas Town Integration Pattern

```go
// In polecat completion handler or witness
raw := exec.Command("bd", "mol", "show", molID, "--json").Output()
summary := callHaiku(buildSummaryPrompt(raw))  // agent's job
exec.Command("bd", "mol", "squash", molID, "--summary", summary).Run()
```

### Why This Design

| Concern | bd's job | Agent's job |
|---------|----------|-------------|
| Store data | ✅ | |
| Query data | ✅ | |
| Generate summaries | | ✅ |
| Call LLMs | | ✅ |
| Manage API keys | | ✅ |

### Implementation Status

- [x] --summary flag added to bd mol squash
- [x] Tests for agent-provided summary
- [ ] Gas Town integration (separate task)

### Acceptance Criteria

- ✅ bd mol squash --summary uses provided text
- ✅ Without --summary, falls back to concatenation
- ✅ No LLM calls in bd itself","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:00.732749-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:29:16.288713-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:29:16.288713-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented --summary flag for agent-provided summaries. bd stays a pure tool; agents provide the intelligence.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:00.733264-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:22.698686-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vh3.5","title":"Tier 4: Auto-squash on molecule completion","description":"Automatically squash molecules when they reach terminal state.

## Integration Points

1. Hook into molecule completion handler
2. Detect when all steps are done/failed
3. Trigger squash automatically

## Config

bd config set mol.auto_squash true  # Default: false
bd config set mol.auto_squash_on_success true  # Only on success
bd config set mol.auto_squash_delay '5m'  # Wait before squash

## Implementation Options

### Option A: Post-Completion Hook
In mol completion handler:
- Check if auto_squash enabled
- Call Squash() after terminal state

### Option B: Git Hook
In .beads/hooks/post-commit:
- bd mol squash --auto

### Option C: Daemon Background Task
- Daemon periodically checks for squashable molecules
- Squashes in background

## Acceptance Criteria

- Completed molecules auto-squash without manual intervention
- Configurable delay before squash
- Option to squash only on success vs always
- Works with both daemon and no-daemon modes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:13.345577-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:40:39.794527-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:40:39.794527-08:00","close_reason":"Won't fix - cleanup handled via molecule workflows (e.g. daily cleanup plugin)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:13.346152-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:22.797141-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2vh3.6","title":"Tier 5 (Future): JSONL archive rotation","description":"Periodic rotation of issues.jsonl for long-running repos.

## Design

.beads/
├── issues.jsonl           # Current (hot)
├── archive/
│   ├── issues-2025-12.jsonl.gz  # Archived (cold)
│   └── ...
└── index.jsonl            # Merged index for queries

## Commands

bd archive rotate [flags]
  --older-than N    Archive issues closed > N days
  --compress        Gzip archives
  --dry-run         Preview

bd archive list           # Show archived periods
bd archive restore <period>  # Restore from archive

## Config

bd config set archive.enabled true
bd config set archive.rotate_days 90
bd config set archive.compress true
bd config set archive.path '.beads/archive'

## Considerations

- Archives can be gitignored (local only) or committed (shared)
- Query layer must check index, hydrate from archive
- Cold storage tiering (S3/GCS) for enterprise
- Merkle proofs preserved for audit

## Priority

This is post-1.0 work. Current focus is on squash (removes ephemeral).
Archive helps with long-term history but is less critical.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:38.210008-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:38.210008-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:38.210543-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}

 {"id":"bd-313v","title":"rpc: Rich mutation events not emitted","description":"The activity command (activity.go) references rich mutation event types (MutationBonded, MutationSquashed, MutationBurned, MutationStatus) that include metadata like OldStatus, NewStatus, ParentID, and StepCount.

However, the emitMutation() function in server_core.go:141 only accepts (eventType, issueID) and only populates Type, IssueID, and Timestamp. The additional metadata fields are never set.

Need to either:
1. Add an emitRichMutation() function that accepts the additional metadata
2. Update call sites (close, bond, squash, burn operations) to emit rich events

Without this fix, the activity feed will never show:
- Status transitions (in_progress -> closed)
- Bonded events with step counts
- Parent molecule relationships

Discovered during code review of bd-xo1o implementation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T04:06:17.39523-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T04:13:19.205249-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T04:13:19.205249-08:00","close_reason":"Added emitRichMutation() function and updated handleClose/handleUpdate to emit MutationStatus events with old/new status metadata. Added comprehensive tests."}

 {"id":"bd-66l4","title":"Runtime bonding: bd mol attach","description":"Attach a molecule to an already-running workflow.

COMMAND: bd mol attach <epic-id> <proto> [--after <issue-id>]

BEHAVIOR:
- Resolve running epic and proto
- Spawn proto as new subtree
- Wire to specified attachment point (or epic root)
- Handle in-progress issues: new work doesn't block completed work

USE CASES:
- Discovered need for docs while implementing feature
- Hotfix needs attaching to release workflow
- Additional testing scope identified mid-flight

FLAGS:
- --after ISSUE: Specific attachment point within epic
- --type: sequential (default) or parallel
- --var: Variables for the attached proto

CONSIDERATIONS:
- What if epic is already closed? Error or reopen?
- What if attachment point issue is closed? Attach as ready-to-work?","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:16.920483-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T01:08:43.530597-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T01:08:43.530597-08:00","close_reason":"Merged into bd-o91r: bond command handles all bonding cases polymorphically","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66l4","depends_on_id":"bd-o5xe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.435542-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-66l4","depends_on_id":"bd-o91r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.813782-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-687g","title":"Code review: mol squash deletion bypasses tombstone system","description":"The deleteEphemeralChildren function in mol_squash.go uses DeleteIssue directly instead of the proper deletion flow. This bypasses tombstone creation, deletion tracking (deletions.jsonl), and dependency cleanup. Could cause issues with deletion propagation across clones.

Current code uses d.DeleteIssue(ctx, id) but should probably use d.DeleteIssues(ctx, ids, false, true, false) for proper tombstone handling.

Alternative: Document that ephemeral issues intentionally use hard delete since they are transient and should never propagate to other clones anyway.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:57:20.223345-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:17:38.073899-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:17:38.073899-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented ephemeral issue filtering from JSONL export and fixed comments leak in DeleteIssue"}
-{"id":"bd-687v","title":"Consider caching external dep resolution results","description":"Each call to GetReadyWork re-checks all external dependencies by:
1. Querying for external deps in the local database
2. Opening each external project's database
3. Querying for closed issues with provides: labels

For workloads with many external deps or slow external databases, this adds latency on every bd ready call.

Potential optimizations:
- In-memory TTL cache for external dep status (e.g., 60 second TTL)
- Store resolved status in a local cache table with timestamp
- Batch resolution of common project/capability pairs

This is not urgent - current implementation is correct and performant for typical workloads. Only becomes an issue with many external deps across many projects.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:16.360877-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:16.360877-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-687v","depends_on_id":"bd-zmmy","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:16.361493-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-68e4","title":"doctor --fix should export when DB has more issues than JSONL","description":"When 'bd doctor' detects a count mismatch (DB has more issues than JSONL), it currently recommends 'bd sync --import-only', which imports JSONL into DB. But JSONL is the source of truth, not the DB.

**Current behavior:**
- Doctor detects: DB has 355 issues, JSONL has 292
- Recommends: 'bd sync --import-only' 
- User runs it: Returns '0 created, 0 updated' (no-op, because JSONL hasn't changed)
- User is stuck

**Root cause:**
The doctor fix is one-directional (JSONL→DB) when it should be bidirectional. If DB has MORE issues, they haven't been exported yet - the fix should be 'bd export' (DB→JSONL), not import.

**Desired fix:**
In fix.DBJSONLSync(), detect which has more data:
- If DB > JSONL: Run 'bd export' to sync JSONL (since DB is the working copy)
- If JSONL > DB: Run 'bd sync --import-only' to import (JSONL is source of truth)
- If equal but timestamps differ: Detect based on file mtime

This makes 'bd doctor --fix' actually fix the problem instead of being a no-op.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T11:17:20.994319182-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:23:24.38523731-07:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:23:24.38523731-07:00"}

 {"id":"bd-fu83","title":"Fix daemon/direct mode inconsistency in relate and duplicate commands","description":"The relate.go and duplicate.go commands have inconsistent daemon/direct mode handling:

When daemonClient is connected, they resolve IDs via RPC but then perform updates directly via store.UpdateIssue(), bypassing the daemon.

Affected locations:
- relate.go:125-139 (runRelate update)
- relate.go:235-246 (runUnrelate update)  
- duplicate.go:120 (runDuplicate update)
- duplicate.go:207 (runSupersede update)

Should either use RPC for updates when daemon is running, or document why direct access is intentional.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T20:52:54.164189-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:47:14.10222-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:47:14.10222-08:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in commit 3ec517cc: relate, unrelate, duplicate, supersede now use RPC Update when daemon available"}

-{"id":"bd-g4b4","title":"bd close hooks: context check and notifications","description":"Add hook system to bd close for context checking and notifications.

## Concept

Register hooks that run on every bd close:

# .beads/config.yaml
hooks:
  on_close:
    - command: 'gt context-check'
    - notify: 'Next step ready'

## Use cases

### Context check on close
After closing a step, check if context is getting full.
If >80%, output warning suggesting session cycling.

### Next step notification  
Automatically show next ready step (complements --continue).

### Custom actions
User-defined scripts for workflow automation.

## Hook types

- command: Run shell command with env vars (BEAD_ID, BEAD_TITLE, etc)
- notify: Send message to agent
- webhook: POST to URL (future)

## Integration with context detection

The context-check hook could:
1. Capture tmux pane (if in tmux session)
2. Estimate context usage (turn count, output length)
3. If high, output: 'Context at ~85%. Consider cycling.'

## Priority
P2 - nice to have, not blocking launch.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T17:03:56.183461-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T17:25:17.139071-08:00"}
+{"id":"bd-g4b4","title":"bd close hooks: context check and notifications","description":"Add hook system to bd close for notifications and custom actions.

## Scope (MVP)

Implement **command hooks only** for bd close. Deferred: notify, webhook types.

## Implementation

### 1. Config Schema

Add to internal/configfile/config.go:

```go
type HooksConfig struct {
    OnClose []HookEntry `yaml:"on_close,omitempty"`
}

type HookEntry struct {
    Command string `yaml:"command"`           // Shell command to run
    Name    string `yaml:"name,omitempty"`    // Optional display name
}
```

Add `Hooks HooksConfig` field to Config struct.

### 2. Hook Execution

Create internal/hooks/close_hooks.go:

```go
func RunCloseHooks(ctx context.Context, cfg *configfile.Config, issue *types.Issue) error {
    for _, hook := range cfg.Hooks.OnClose {
        cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "sh", "-c", hook.Command)
        cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(),
            "BEAD_ID="+issue.ID,
            "BEAD_TITLE="+issue.Title,
            "BEAD_TYPE="+string(issue.IssueType),
            "BEAD_PRIORITY="+strconv.Itoa(issue.Priority),
            "BEAD_CLOSE_REASON="+issue.CloseReason,
        )
        cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
        cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
        if err := cmd.Run(); err \!= nil {
            // Log warning but dont fail the close
            fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: close hook %q failed: %v\n", hook.Name, err)
        }
    }
    return nil
}
```

### 3. Integration Point

In cmd/bd/close.go, after successful close:

```go
// Run close hooks
if cfg := configfile.Load(); cfg \!= nil {
    hooks.RunCloseHooks(ctx, cfg, closedIssue)
}
```

### 4. Example Config

```yaml
# .beads/config.yaml
hooks:
  on_close:
    - name: show-next
      command: bd ready --limit 1
    - name: context-check  
      command: echo "Issue $BEAD_ID closed. Check context if nearing limit."
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| BEAD_ID | Issue ID (e.g., bd-abc1) |
| BEAD_TITLE | Issue title |
| BEAD_TYPE | Issue type (task, bug, feature, etc.) |
| BEAD_PRIORITY | Priority (0-4) |
| BEAD_CLOSE_REASON | Close reason if provided |

## Testing

Add test in internal/hooks/close_hooks_test.go:
- Test hook execution with mock config
- Test env vars are set correctly
- Test hook failure doesnt block close

## Files to Create/Modify

1. **Create:** internal/hooks/close_hooks.go
2. **Create:** internal/hooks/close_hooks_test.go  
3. **Modify:** internal/configfile/config.go (add HooksConfig)
4. **Modify:** cmd/bd/close.go (call RunCloseHooks)
5. **Modify:** docs/CONFIG.md (document hooks config)

## Out of Scope (Future)

- notify hook type (gt mail integration)
- webhook type (HTTP POST)
- on_create, on_update hooks
- Hook timeout configuration
- Parallel hook execution","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T17:03:56.183461-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:27:35.531819-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-zmmy","title":"bd ready resolves external dependencies","description":"Extend bd ready to check external blocked_by references:

1. Parse external:<project>:<capability> from blocked_by
2. Look up project path from external_projects config
3. Check if target project has provides:<capability> label on a closed issue
4. If not satisfied, issue is blocked

Example output:
```bash
bd ready
# gt-xyz: blocked by external:beads:mol-run-assignee (not provided)
# gt-abc: ready
```

Depends on: bd-om4a (external: prefix), bd-66w1 (config)

Part of cross-project dependency system.
See: gastown/docs/cross-project-deps.md","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T22:37:50.03794-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T23:42:25.042402-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T23:42:25.042402-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented external dep resolution in GetReadyWork - filters issues with unsatisfied external:project:capability deps","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zmmy","depends_on_id":"bd-om4a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T22:38:38.106657-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-zmmy","depends_on_id":"bd-66w1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T22:38:38.175633-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-zt59","title":"Deferred HOP schema additions (P2/P3)","description":"Deferred from bd-7pwh after review. Add when semantics are clearer and actually needed:

- assignee_ref: Structured EntityRef alongside string assignee
- work_type: 'mutex' vs 'open_competition' (everything is mutex in v0.1)
- crystallizes: bool for work that compounds vs evaporates (can derive from issue_type)
- cross_refs: URIs to beads in other repos (needs federation first)
- skill_vector: []float32 embeddings placeholder (YAGNI)

These can be added later without breaking changes (all optional fields).","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T17:54:20.02496-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T17:54:20.02496-08:00"}
-{"id":"bd-zw72","title":"Investigate incremental blocked_issues_cache updates at scale","description":"Current blocked_issues_cache strategy does full DELETE + INSERT on every dependency/status change.

**Problem at scale:**
- 10K issues: ~50ms rebuild (acceptable)
- 100K issues: ~500ms rebuild (noticeable)
- 1M issues: multi-second rebuilds (problematic)

**Current implementation (blocked_cache.go:104-154):**
- DELETE FROM blocked_issues_cache
- INSERT with recursive CTE

**Potential optimizations:**
1. **Incremental updates:** Only add/remove affected issue IDs instead of full rebuild
2. **Dirty tracking:** Skip rebuild if cache is already valid
3. **Async rebuild:** Rebuild in background, serve stale cache briefly
4. **Partial invalidation:** Only invalidate affected subtree

**Decision needed:** Is this premature optimization? Current target is <100K issues.

**Benchmark:** Add benchmark for cache rebuild at 100K scale to measure actual impact.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:55.165718-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:55.165718-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zw72","depends_on_id":"bd-h0we","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:55.166427-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

- cross_refs: URIs to beads in other repos (needs federation first)
- skill_vector: []float32 embeddings placeholder (YAGNI)

These can be added later without breaking changes (all optional fields).","status":"deferred","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T17:54:20.02496-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:27:02.445219-08:00"}
+{"id":"bd-zw72","title":"Investigate incremental blocked_issues_cache updates at scale","description":"Current blocked_issues_cache strategy does full DELETE + INSERT on every dependency/status change.

**Problem at scale:**
- 10K issues: ~50ms rebuild (acceptable)
- 100K issues: ~500ms rebuild (noticeable)
- 1M issues: multi-second rebuilds (problematic)

**Current implementation (blocked_cache.go:104-154):**
- DELETE FROM blocked_issues_cache
- INSERT with recursive CTE

**Potential optimizations:**
1. **Incremental updates:** Only add/remove affected issue IDs instead of full rebuild
2. **Dirty tracking:** Skip rebuild if cache is already valid
3. **Async rebuild:** Rebuild in background, serve stale cache briefly
4. **Partial invalidation:** Only invalidate affected subtree

**Decision needed:** Is this premature optimization? Current target is <100K issues.

**Benchmark:** Add benchmark for cache rebuild at 100K scale to measure actual impact.","status":"deferred","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:55.165718-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:27:02.297059-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zw72","depends_on_id":"bd-h0we","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:55.166427-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
